articles.title: ADOBE ADDS NEW AI-POWERED IMAGE-EDITING FEATURES TO PHOTOSHOP
articles.summary: Adobe rolled out new AI-powered image-editing features to Photoshop using its Firefly models, including generative upscaling, object blending, and enhanced object removal. The Generative Upscale feature enhances images up to 8 megapixels, while Harmonize helps blend new objects into scenes by adjusting lighting and colors. The improved object-removal tool now focuses specifically on removing objects and filling gaps with an appropriate background rather than considering the entire scene.

articles.title: INTRODUCING RUNWAY ALEPH
articles.summary: Runway's Aleph is a state-of-the-art in-context video model that enables comprehensive editing, transformation, and generation of video content. The system performs multi-task visual generation, including adding or removing objects, changing camera angles, modifying lighting, and applying style transfers. Early access will begin with Enterprise and Creative Partners, and broader availability is planned for all users soon.

articles.title: LEAKER SHARES CLEAREST LOOK YET AT ALL THE IPHONE 17 DUMMY UNITS
articles.summary: Recent leaks, including a new image from reliable leaker Sonny Dickson, offer the clearest look yet at the iPhone 17 lineup's color options, revealing bold new shades like navy blue and orange for the Pro models—a departure from Apple's typically muted tones. The image also shows expected colors for the iPhone 17 Air and regular models, with options like light blue, light pink, and light gold, giving users a good idea of what to expect ahead of the official launch.

articles.title: TOP 10 UI ANNOYANCES
articles.summary: Jakob Nielsen identifies common usability problems as expensive business failures rather than minor user complaints. The analysis covers ten specific UI annoyances plus six additional issues that frustrate users daily. These design flaws compound over time, causing user abandonment and lasting damage to brand trust.

articles.title: PROMPT BUILDING USER INTERFACES
articles.summary: AI products struggle with discoverability as most interfaces are empty text fields that don't help users understand capabilities or achieve optimal results. Bench is an agent workspace for teams that evolved from role-based prompt suggestions to atomic, combinable suggestions that can be stitched together through a task builder interface. This prompt-building approach clarifies product capabilities, surfaces new functionality, rewrites prompts for better outcomes, and guides effective task completion.

articles.title: IT'S NOT YOU: YOUR UX DESIGN JOB IS FRUSTRATING AND UNFULFILLING
articles.summary: UX designers, despite working in high-paying, respected roles, often face alienation through rigid Agile processes, performance metrics, and extreme task specialization that limit their autonomy, creativity, and connection to their work. Instead of crafting holistic, meaningful experiences, designers are frequently reduced to optimizing isolated UI elements, leading to stress, a loss of fulfilment, and disconnection from their human potential.

articles.title: OPEN-SOURCE, BROWSER-BASED 3D CAD APPLICATION
articles.summary: Chili3D delivers near-native performance for powerful online modeling, editing, and rendering—all without local installation.

articles.title: YOUR SMART CREATIVE AGENT
articles.summary: Pippit AI is an AI-powered platform that enables you to create videos, images, ads, and avatars with ease.

articles.title: DEPOSITPHOTOS AI IMAGE GENERATOR
articles.summary: Depositphotos' AI image generator converts text to an image in just a few clicks. Elevate your projects with unique, licensed pictures to stand out online.

articles.title: VIBE CODING: NOT WORTH THE RISK?
articles.summary: Recent security breaches at Tea, a dating app, and Replit's AI coding platform expose the dangers of rapid, AI-assisted "vibe coding" development. Tea exposed 72,000 user images, including government IDs, in publicly accessible databases, while Replit's AI agent deleted production data despite explicit freeze orders. The incidents reveal how prioritizing speed over security in democratized coding tools creates catastrophic vulnerabilities that harm real users.

articles.title: WHY I THINK V0 IS A GREAT PROTOTYPING TOOL FOR DESIGNERS
articles.summary: Vercel's v0 excels at rapid prototyping by bridging traditional design-development handoff gaps through realistic, interactive code generation. The GenUI tool creates functional prototypes in minutes but requires precise prompting techniques to prevent unwanted modifications during iteration. This technology shifts designers from artifact creators toward system directors who guide AI-generated interfaces and focus on user experience strategy.

articles.title: WITHOUT CREATES NEW BRAND FOR SLEEPOVER AIRPORT NAP PODS
articles.summary: Design studio Without developed the new visual and verbal identity for “Sleepover”, a global airport service offering travelers restful spaces like nap pods and flexi-cabins, replacing the more utilitarian “Sleep n' Fly” brand to create a more emotionally engaging and scalable experience. With playful mascots, a calming purple palette, and a name aimed at turning airport stress into comfort, the identity is designed to stand out in chaotic terminals while feeling approachable and universally understandable.

articles.title: NINE LUXURY COLOR PALETTES THAT CAPTURE HIGH-END DESIGN IN 2025
articles.summary: Nine luxury color palettes for 2025 featuring rich jewel tones, sumptuous neutrals, and metallic accents that elevate high-end design projects.

articles.title: TAMAGOTCHI RE-DESIGNS, VIDEO GAME VISUALS AND LUCKY CHARMS: WELCOME TO THE OVERSTIMULATING 3D WORLD OF IOSIF ABAAB
articles.summary: Berlin-based 3D designer Iosif Abaab creates overstimulating, hyperreal visuals by obsessively adding colorful, shiny details to ordinary objects.
